symptom: months of belly pain and low grade fevers after an operation that was supposed to fix everything
hook: there was a full metal surgical clamp still sitting inside the abdomen where an instrument should never be
whatYouSee: A bright metal surgical clamp with ringed finger handles and a hinged jaw sits inside the abdomen overlying the bowel where no instrument should ever remain.
whyItMatters: An instrument left behind after surgery keeps irritating the tissue and can drive infection or perforation or an abscess until it comes out.
treatment: It is removed with another operation to retrieve the instrument and clean out any infection it caused.
takeaway: A whole surgical instrument on an abdominal film was left behind at a previous operation and needs to come back out.
laypersonQuestion: Does that shape look like a tool to you?
seedHint: Look at the bright object in the middle and the shape of its handles.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with months of belly pain and low grade fevers after an operation that was supposed to fix everything.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And there was a full metal surgical clamp still sitting inside the abdomen where an instrument should never be.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Retained surgical instrument

👀 What you see:
A bright metal surgical clamp with ringed finger handles and a hinged jaw sits inside the abdomen overlying the bowel where no instrument should ever remain.

🦴 Why it matters:
An instrument left behind after surgery keeps irritating the tissue and can drive infection or perforation or an abscess until it comes out.

💊 Treatment:
It is removed with another operation to retrieve the instrument and clean out any infection it caused.
